what_it_does: Serializes the C2 node-type library (NodeType) into the JSON shape served by the /object_info endpoint, so a generic frontend can build its palette, renderers, and inspectors without per-type code.
observations: Defines a Pydantic model ObjectInfoEntry that mirrors the fields a generic frontend needs from NodeType.
Keeps SCHEMA_VER as a separate additive serialization-version constant, distinct from individual node-type versions.
Uses the node-type's registry key as the dict key and does not repeat name inside the entry.
Supplies default_factory defaults for optional schema/inspector/action fields to support additive schema evolution.
build_object_info validates that every registry value is a NodeType and that the registry key matches nt.name, failing loudly otherwise.
Outputs a plain JSON-serializable dict via model_dump(mode='json').
standouts: Additive schema versioning (SCHEMA_VER) decouples backend serialization changes from frontend readiness.
Fail-loud registry validation enforces the single-source rule at serialization time.
The design assumes a fully generic frontend driven entirely by the serialized type library.
conventions: Follows the project's C2/C5 contract-layer naming and references rule 3 (single source) and rule 4 (fail loud).
Uses Pydantic BaseModel for serialization and validation.
Prefers explicit type hints and docstrings on module, class, and functions.
concerns: build_object_info is annotated to return a plain dict rather than a more precise type such as dict[str, ObjectInfoEntry] or a typed wrapper.
The validation only checks nt.name when it is truthy; an empty-string name silently bypasses the key-name consistency check.
notes: This is the bridge file: it turns the runtime node-type registry into the frontend's entire type catalog, with versioning and strict validation.
questions: What exactly does the Ports type contain, and is it itself Pydantic-serializable?
How and where is the node_types registry populated before being passed here?
What does the C5 spec prescribe beyond the fields already serialized?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it generates a versioned, additive, fail-loud serialization of the registry for a generic frontend.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for node types
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the frontend completely data-driven: add a node type to the registry and it appears in the UI automatically, with backend and frontend able to evolve at different speeds.
novelty: The explicit SCHEMA_VER constant that signals additive serialization shape changes separately from per-node versions, paired with registry-key/name consistency checks.
contribution: Provides the serialization schema and the validated builder that produces the /object_info payload from the node-type registry, enabling a generic frontend to consume node types without bespoke per-type code.
summary_for_embedding: Serializes the node-type registry into a JSON-ready /object_info map for a generic frontend.
intention_for_embedding: To let the frontend discover and render node types without per-type code by consuming a stable, versioned, additive serialization of the contract library.

symbols.does: Pydantic model that defines the serialized representation of one node type for the frontend.
symbols.description: ObjectInfoEntry maps selected NodeType fields (title, category, kind, ports, schemas, render sets, actions, version) into a JSON-serializable object. The node name is omitted because it serves as the outer dict key.

symbols.does: Factory method that constructs an ObjectInfoEntry from a NodeType instance.
symbols.description: from_node_type copies each relevant NodeType attribute into the Pydantic model, converting set-like collections to lists so the result is JSON-serializable.

symbols.does: Validates the node-type registry and builds the /object_info response dict.
symbols.description: build_object_info iterates over the registry, ensures every value is a NodeType and that each key matches the type's own name, then serializes each entry to JSON-ready dict form.
